verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Strategy chosen + justified; Trade-offs recorded; Prototype path identified; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gate executed red with the decision artifact absent (3/3 failing), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.strategy_evidence: planning-spine-v0/docs/portable_release_packaging_decision.json adopts a hybrid: static musl (the flake's flexnetosMuslToolchain, proven present) for the eligible binary surface, and a relocatable extracted closure bound at a private /nix by the shipped yzx-envelope for the full stack (GUI/Tauri/WebKitGTK, PostgreSQL/RuVector) — justified against this session's live evidence rather than abstract preference.
gate_result.trade_off_evidence: Five candidates each carry a substantive pro/con verdict: nix bundle (rejected primary — multi-GB self-extract, opaque to byte capture), static-musl-everything (rejected full — would silently drop the Glass front door, a forbidden downgrade), relocatable extracted closure (adopted — exact tested store paths travel, run unpatched inside the envelope), nix-user-chroot (rejected — blocked by the kernel.apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns denial proven live during ARCHBP-065, with no profiled fallback), host-Nix (dev-time only per I09/I17).
gate_result.prototype_evidence: The path lands on ARCHBP-021 (YZXCONV-021) in five steps: musl proof for the eligible set, explicit classification of the non-musl remainder, closure extraction via nix path-info -r, a yzx-envelope launcher with --ro-bind <bundle>/store /nix reusing the proven executor fallback chain, and a moved-prefix relocation test tied to CT-G6; bound to invariants I09/I17 and the t10-3/t10-4 gauntlets.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The decision is grounded in executed evidence from this session: the ARCHBP-065 bwrap AppArmor denial (recorded executor downgrade), the ARCHBP-130 envelope latency benchmark (~28ms setup, −0.04% compute overhead — making the envelope launcher a viable release vehicle), and the flake's existing musl cross toolchain.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-134 is complete: the portable-release packaging strategy is decided — hybrid static-musl + relocatable extracted closure launched through the yzx-envelope — with all five candidate mechanisms' trade-offs recorded, nix-user-chroot rejected on a live-proven AppArmor finding, and a five-step prototype path identified that lands on ARCHBP-021 under invariants I09/I17.
